name: Beet Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 Dried Boletus Mushrooms (OR 1/2 lb Chopped Mushrooms)
Liquid from the Cooked Mushrooms, Optional
1 quart Beet Kvas (OR 1 tsp Sour Salt with Crystalized Citric Acid)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak boletus mushrooms overnight.
2. Cook in a little water until tender, then cool and chop finely.
3. Scrub beets and cut into quarters. Cover with water and cook over low heat until tender, about 1 to 2 hours. Cool and strain the liquid, reserving it. Slip off the peels (wear rubber gloves to prevent purple hands). This can be done up to a day in advance.
4. Peel and cut up the other vegetables. Add the bay leaf, peppercorns, and boletus or mushrooms to the vegetables with enough water to cover, and cook over low heat until tender in a large aluminum pot.
5. Strain the beet liquid into the vegetables. Shred the beets in a processor or on a medium grater and add.
6. Simmer for about 10 minutes and strain again into a large pot.
7. Do not press the vegetables to keep the broth clear.
8. Add the beet kvas, mushroom liquid, pepper, and salt. Bring to a gentle boil, then turn off the heat.
9. Taste; the flavor should be tart, mellow, and full. For more tanginess, add fresh lemon juice or sour salt.
10. Keep well in the refrigerator. Reheat gently; do not overcook as the color may darken.
11. To serve, pour over 3 or 4 vushka (dumplings) in soup plates and garnish with fresh dill.
